notes: You need to use Alt-Tab to get past the initial black screen. Game play fine.
title: Tomb Raider VI: The Angel of Darkness

notes: I created a new report because I don't know if is possible to edit one. I played this game for 28 hours and finished it, the only problem is the cut scenes that don't play, but only video cut scenes, not in-game rendered ones, I forgot to say that in my first report. Because of this problem, sometimes you will get a back screen and nothing will happen (when the game starts for example), just press space or esc to skip the cut scene. These videos are playable through a video player directly on Linux, but not inside wine prefix when you are playing, they are inside the folder "data/FMV"
title: Tomb Raider VI: The Angel of Darkness

notes: No intro videos/cut scenes, but the game is playable.
title: Tomb Raider VI: The Angel of Darkness

notes: Except the videos, they are like playing but with no sound and no video, i mean, when the video ends the game continue, if the video's duration is 1 minute, the screen will be black for a minute. You can still skip the video :D
title: Tomb Raider VI: The Angel of Darkness

notes: Works fine out of the box on Proton, but no intro videos. I installed D9VK and it worked great too, even when testing it with the new ACO compiler.
title: Tomb Raider VI: The Angel of Darkness
